description: To refuse: to decline clearly and without aggression. Phi needs this word for consent to mean anything; a yes is only real where a no is sayable. Refusal here is a boundary stated, not a rejection of the person.
sound_symbolism: 'na' plants the no, 'we' softens the ground around it, 'ri' keeps the voice level. Firm at the front, kind after.
grammatical_notes: Verb: 'thia po naweri.' (You can refuse.) In dialogue: 'thia naweri. mia thia sheluo.' (You refuse. I listen to you.)

pillars.peace-linguistics-practices: Nonviolent speech is not agreement; it is honesty without attack. The clear no, respectfully received, is that practice in one word.
